hypothesis: Training ensembles in semi-supervised molecular graph learning with gradients allowed through the consensus target combined with an explicit representation-diversity regularizer (e.g., orthogonality penalty on penultimate-layer activations) will produce individual models with lower test MAE on held-out molecular property benchmarks (e.g., QM9, ZINC) and higher ensemble ambiguity than detached-consensus training, whereas allowing gradients through the consensus without the diversity regularizer will induce learner collusion, reduce ambiguity, and worsen test performance.
